abstract: This research explores the integration of Islamic pedagogical values within the Project for Strengthening the Pancasila Student Profile and Work Culture (P5BK) to refine student character development at SMKN 1 Masbagik. Utilizing a qualitative descriptive approach, data were gathered through semi-structured interviews, field observations, and comprehensive document analysis, subsequently processed using the Miles and Huberman interactive model. The findings reveal that the synergy between P5BK and Islamic education is realized through thematic projects focusing on sustainable lifestyles, employability, and local wisdom. The implementation follows a structured four-stage framework: planning, execution, evaluation, and identifying supportive facilitators. Key advantages of this integrated model include its operational flexibility, strengthened collaboration with the local community and industry, and its ability to provide immersive, real-world experiences that heighten students' social and environmental consciousness. Theoretically, this study reinforces the efficacy of the Independent Curriculum (Kurikulum Merdeka) by demonstrating how value-based frameworks can enhance teacher competence and elevate the overall quality of vocational education.
